title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.

title: Planes fight fire in Fontainebleau forest near Paris
How investigators track the causes of wildfires: 'A snail shell and a stone are valuable clues'
Macron and the military: 10 years made of mistrust, hope, and budget hikes
subhead: Over his two terms, President Emmanuel Macron oversaw a dramatic increase in the defense budget and a major strategic shift in response to new threats.

title: France sanctions Russian elite cyberespionage unit 'Turla'
subhead: France accused Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) of a series of cyberattacks targeting French ministries and a research institute working on sensitive subjects. The Russian ambassador to France will be summoned in the coming days.

title: 'Thank you, dear Hungary, for proving that anything is always possible'
subhead: Jean-Baptiste Chastand, Le Monde's Central Europe correspondent, writes a letter to this 'hostile and surprising' country he has covered for more than 10 years.

title: 'And I thought coke was for stressed-out executives!': Cocaine's ravages among manual workers
subhead: 'Cocaine at work' (3/3). Long associated with elite, urban circles, cocaine has now made its way deep into rural France and its mid-sized towns. On construction sites, farms and in warehouses, workers, some barely out of their teens, are turning to the drug to get through the physical toll of their jobs.

'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

title: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhead: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

title: Simon Abkarian, star of 'De Gaulle': 'At school in Lebanon, my nickname was de Gaulle'
subhead: The actor and theater director plays Charles de Gaulle in the recently released two-part film. In this article, he explains how he prepared for the role of France's World War II hero.

title: French MPs' vote to establish presumption of legitimate defense for police sets dangerous precedent
'The latest chapter in Le Pen's legal saga illustrates the intractable challenge of judging politicians'
'Diverse France, that of Kylian Mbappé and the national team, is too promising a reality to be used as an electoral punching bag'
